expectations.text: Mentions minilm_relation_heads startup validation as a needed fix
expectations.evidence: The baseline analysis focuses on CUDA/CPU issues, bitsandbytes, attention_mask=None fallback, pad_token=EOS corruption, and optimizer waste — none of which are the four Priority 1 fixes from the skill. The minilm_relation_heads validation is never mentioned.

expectations.text: Mentions attention output / attn_implementation validation as a needed fix
expectations.evidence: Does not mention the eager attention requirement or the fact that sdpa/flash_attention_2 would break attention_kl.

expectations.text: Mentions missing __init__.py or namespace package issue
expectations.evidence: No mention of __init__.py or package structure anywhere in the analysis.

expectations.text: Provides specific code snippets or exact fix instructions (not just high-level description)
expectations.evidence: Provides code for attention_mask fallback, loss_balancer conditional inclusion, and save_dir pathlib fix

expectations.text: References specific file paths and approximate line numbers

suggestions.reason: The baseline found legitimate issues (attention_mask=None guard, pad_token=EOS risk, loss_balancer optimizer waste) that the skill should consider adding to its catalog. These are real bugs the skill currently misses.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline found different issues than the skill — it focused on runtime environment concerns (CUDA, bitsandbytes) and code robustness (attention_mask fallback, pad_token) while missing the four defensive startup validations that the skill prioritizes. This validates that the skill adds value beyond what a general code audit would find.
